title: RANKED: World’s top 20 largest gold mines
summary: Gold grabbed the spotlight in 2025 as rising global trade tensions and accelerated central bank buying propelled the metal by more than 60%. Its rise continued into 2026, with prices reaching an all-time peak of nearly $5,600 an ounce in January. Since then, bullion has fallen hard amid a market-wide liquidity stress and rising expectations of high interest rates, which were exacerbated by the war in the Middle East. Since the start of March, gold fell as much as 15%, nearly erasing all of its gains on the year. Gold mining equities, too, fell victim to the rout , with many of the market leaders suffering losses of 20% to 40%. CHART: Billions wiped of mining stocks as gold, silver, copper prices plummet Nevertheless, long run view on the metal remains intact, given that central banks have been building up their gold holdings.
